subheadline: The only software development agents that work everywhere you do. From IDE to CI/CD - delegate complete tasks like refactors, incident response, and migrations to Droids without changing your tools, models, or workflow.
value_proposition: AI coding agents (Droids) that integrate natively into any developer workflow—IDE, CLI, Slack, web, or project manager—to autonomously handle complete software engineering tasks.
target_audience: Software engineering teams and enterprise engineering orgs seeking to delegate coding tasks to AI agents without disrupting existing tooling, models, or workflows.

pricing.model: contact-sales with self-serve CLI entry point
pricing.price_anchoring: No pricing shown on page; enterprise deals implied via 'Contact Sales'; free CLI install lowers top-of-funnel barrier

features: IDE and terminal integration (VS Code, JetBrains, Vim, and more)
Web/desktop interface for no-setup agent delegation
CLI for scripting, parallelizing, and CI/CD pipeline integration
Slack/Teams integration for incident triage and bug fixes in plain English
Project manager integration (auto-trigger agents from issue assignment, generate PRs with full traceability)
differentiators: 'The only software development agents that work everywhere you do'
Interface and vendor agnostic — works with any model provider, any dev tooling, any interface
No workflow disruption: 'without changing your tools, models, or workflow'
'AI that will work with you, not replace you'
Complete task delegation (not just autocomplete): refactors, incident response, migrations
trust_signals: $150M Series C from Khosla Ventures, Sequoia Capital, Blackstone
State-of-the-art security protocols to protect data and IP
Dedicated security and legal pages (Privacy Policy, Terms, SLA, DPA, BAA)

design_patterns.layout: Full-width dark hero with animated character-by-character headline, marquee logo strip, tabbed product feature section (01–05), enterprise two-column section, news card grid, full-width CTA banner, footer
design_patterns.color_scheme: Dark background (near-black), high-contrast white text, likely accent color for CTAs; minimal use of color outside text
design_patterns.typography: Modern sans-serif, large display type for headline, clean body text; animated letter-splitting effect on hero headline
notable_patterns: Numbered tab navigation (01–05) for product use cases
Arrow-right iconography on all CTAs and links
News section styled as editorial cards with 'New' badges

emotional_tone: Confident and aspirational with a technical undercurrent; the 'work with you, not replace you' line adds a reassuring, human touch
key_objections_addressed: Workflow disruption: 'without changing your tools, models, or workflow'
Vendor lock-in: 'interface and vendor agnostic, working with any model provider'
Job displacement fear: 'AI that will work with you, not replace you'
Setup friction: free CLI one-liner and 'no setup required' web option lower adoption barrier
missing_or_weak: Only 3 customer logos — thin social proof for an enterprise-positioned product
Zero customer testimonials or quotes anywhere on the page
No demo video or product screenshot in hero
No quantified outcomes (e.g., '40% faster PRs', 'reduced MTTR by X')
Pricing page linked in nav but no pricing shown — creates uncertainty for self-serve buyers
No free trial or freemium tier called out explicitly
News section doubles as social proof but reads more like a blog — weaker than case studies
standout_elements: Bold, falsifiable 'The only' claim anchors differentiation immediately
Omnichannel coverage (IDE + web + CLI + Slack + PM) is unusually comprehensive and well-organized
'AI that will work with you, not replace you' directly defuses the most common emotional objection to AI coding tools
$150M Series C announcement integrated into news section adds real-time credibility
Numbered 01–05 product section makes complex multi-surface product easy to scan
curl install command in hero signals developer-first authenticity
